raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Endorsing and transmitting a graded hierarchy of sins differentiated by motivating passion, via appeal to philosophical authority",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Sins possess objective degrees of magnitude that can be compared",
    "Lust as a motive produces greater sin than anger",
    "Philosophical discernment (distinct from vulgar comparison) can validly rank spiritual faults",
    "Theophrastus supplies reliable insight into non-physical moral order"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent within a framework that accepts both qualitative distinctions among passions and quantitative gradations of sin",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "A shared metaphysical ontology in which passions are real forces, sins have measurable spiritual weight, and moral discrimination aids ethical or soteriological progress",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Absence of objective sin gradations, misattribution to Theophrastus, or application that justifies self-exculpation by reclassifying motives"
}
